one_line: An artisan-scribe of the Indus cities, carving tiny steatite seals for traders and officials in the Mature Harappan period.

appearance.age_look: mid-20s, lean and wiry from careful handwork rather than heavy labor
appearance.face: oval face, medium-brown Indus Valley complexion, straight nose, strong brows, deep-set sharp eyes, a few tiny stone-dust nicks on the cheeks, default expression focused and calm with a hint of quiet pride.
appearance.hair: thick straight black hair oiled and neatly tied into a low bun at the back of the head, front kept smooth with a slight center part; no hat, but a slim dark-brown headband of twisted cord holding back wisps.
appearance.costume: Knee-length wrapped cotton tunic in undyed off‑white, V-neck and straight half sleeves, worn over a plain mid-brown woven cloth dhoti wrapped tightly to mid-calf and knotted at the waist, with a narrow dark indigo border along the hem; a soft dusty-terracotta shawl lightly draped from left shoulder across the torso and hanging behind, practical to fold over the hands; simple flat leather sandals; jewellery limited to a single short carnelian bead necklace (small cylindrical beads) and one copper finger ring on the right hand; fabrics look hand‑loomed with very fine, subtle horizontal weaving, no bold patterns.
appearance.signature: A small square off-white steatite seal (about 3 cm) held delicately in the left hand, showing a classic one-horned bull-like animal standing before a ritual stand above a short row of tiny, incised Indus signs, with a slender copper engraving chisel and stone burnisher gripped in the right hand.

ref_desc: A lean mid-20s Harappan seal cutter with medium-brown skin, low black hair bun and cord headband, in an off-white tunic, brown dhoti with dark indigo border, dusty-terracotta shawl and simple beads, holding a small square unicorn steatite seal with copper chisel and burnisher.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a Mature Harappan seal cutter: a lean young adult with medium-brown Indus Valley complexion, oval face, serious sharp eyes and low black hair bun held by a slim dark-brown cord headband. He wears an off-white knee-length wrapped cotton tunic with V-neck and straight half sleeves over a plain mid-brown tightly wrapped dhoti that reaches mid-calf, both fabrics looking finely hand-loomed, the dhoti hem edged with a narrow dark indigo border. A dusty-terracotta shawl is draped from his left shoulder across his torso and hanging behind for practical use, and he stands in simple flat leather sandals. His only jewellery is a short necklace of small cylindrical carnelian beads and a single copper ring on his right finger. In his hands he displays his tools: in the left, a small square off-white steatite seal showing a one-horned bull-like animal before a stand with a short row of tiny, undeciphered Indus signs, and in the right, a slender copper engraving chisel and smooth stone burnisher. Use a color palette of off-white, dusty terracotta, dark indigo, medium brown and copper, and show him from multiple angles to emphasize the consistent hair bun, headband, garments, shawl, necklace and seal-carving tools.
